title: Add --policy flag to buildah pull
body:
This allows the user to specify the pull policy for pulling images.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

Please label this pull request according to what type of issue you are
addressing, especially if this is a release targeted pull request.

Uncomment only one `/kind <>` line, hit enter to put that in a new line, and
remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->

> /kind bug
> /kind cleanup
> /kind deprecation
> /kind design
> /kind documentation

> /kind feature

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@TomSweeneyRedHat @vrothberg @nalind @giuseppe PTAL

I think I have hit all of the pull policy tests.

I've played with it a bit this morning looks good.  Just a couple of added checks in the current tests would be nice.
I also noted you declared "PullIfNewer" in the policy, but didn't include it as an option.  I'm assuming based on discussions in the podman pull PR?  (https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8301)
Yes.  I don't believe it does anything.

Also cleaned up some more wrapping in buildah. Errors went from

```
$ /bin/buildah inspect docker.io/library/alpine1
error reading build object "docker.io/library/alpine1": error reading image: error locating image "docker.io/library/alpine1" for importing settings: error locating image with name "docker.io/library/alpine1" ([docker.io/library/alpine1]): image not known
ERRO exit status 125 
```
alpine1 mentioned 4 times.
to
```                             
$ ./bin/buildah inspect docker.io/library/alpine1
importing settings: error locating image with name "docker.io/library/alpine1" ([docker.io/library/alpine1]): image not known
ERRO exit status 125                      
```
Only mentioned 2 times.
@edsantiago @TomSweeneyRedHat @nalind @vrothberg Lets get this in, we want to release buildah 1.18.0 today.
@rhatdan does this need a rebase or is it good to `/lgtm` at this point?
